evals.name: add-missing-repo-on-disk
evals.expected_output: Skill scans <workspace.coding>/, finds samba-onboarding/ on disk but no matching row in <indexes.code_projects>. Proposes ADD via AskUserQuestion. After approval, appends a row to the index file with Path=<workspace.coding>/samba-onboarding, Status=active, GitHub detected from `git remote get-url origin` (the-sid-dani/samba-onboarding), Stack and Brief left as (needs review).
preconditions: Real state on Sid's machine (2026-05-17): <workspace.coding>/samba-onboarding/ exists as a real git repo with origin pointing at the-sid-dani/samba-onboarding.
Index file <indexes.code_projects> has only the trf-benchmark-dashboard row (no samba-onboarding row).

assertions.text: Output identifies samba-onboarding as an ADD candidate
Output asks via AskUserQuestion before mutating the index file
After approval, <indexes.code_projects> contains a row with the path beru-workspace/2-Coding/samba-onboarding (or equivalent token-resolved path)
GitHub field on the new row is the-sid-dani/samba-onboarding (detected via git remote)
Stack and Brief fields contain '(needs review)' literal — no fabricated values

evals.name: flag-row-pointing-to-missing-folder
evals.expected_output: Skill scans disk + parses index. Index has a row pointing to a folder that doesn't exist on disk (test fixture: pre-add a row with path '<workspace.coding>/ghost-repo' before invoking, where ghost-repo/ does NOT exist on disk). Skill proposes FLAG, asks user via AskUserQuestion (flag-with-MISSING-status vs remove-row), applies approved choice. Default action is flag (preserves audit trail).
preconditions: Test fixture: pre-add a row '| ghost-repo | <workspace.coding>/ghost-repo | TS | active | the-sid-dani/ghost-repo | A test repo | 2026-05-01 |' to <indexes.code_projects> BEFORE running the eval. The folder ghost-repo/ must NOT exist on disk.

assertions.text: Output identifies ghost-repo as MISSING (folder gone)
Output asks via AskUserQuestion whether to flag (preserve row) or remove (delete row)
Default behavior preserves the row (flagging is non-destructive)
After 'flag' approval, the row's Status field reads 'MISSING' in <indexes.code_projects>
Skill does NOT silently delete rows without explicit user approval

evals.name: no-drift-exit-cleanly
evals.expected_output: Disk and index already match. Skill scans, finds no ADDs or MISSINGs, prints '✨ Index in sync. <N> code repos tracked, all present on disk.' and exits without asking any questions or mutating any files.
preconditions: Disk and index are in sync — every directory under <workspace.coding>/ has a matching row in <indexes.code_projects>, every row in the index points to an existing folder.

assertions.text: Output reports 'in sync' or equivalent (no drift detected)
Output does NOT invoke AskUserQuestion (no decisions to make)
Output reports a count of total tracked repos
